codeReview.summary: Failed-login recording exists, but throttling behavior does not match the feature/contract requirements. The implementation enforces a 30s/60s/lockout policy (3/5/10 attempts) instead of the required 1s/2s/5s policy (3/10/20 attempts), and tests codify the wrong behavior.

issues.description: Throttle thresholds and durations are incorrect for this feature: code uses 3->30s, 5->60s, 10->lockout (`LOGIN_THROTTLE_DELAY_1_SECONDS = 30`, `LOGIN_THROTTLE_DELAY_2_ATTEMPTS = 5`, `LOGIN_THROTTLE_DELAY_2_SECONDS = 60`, `LOGIN_LOCKOUT_ATTEMPTS = 10`) instead of required 3->1s, 10->2s, 20->5s in a 24h window.

Throttled requests are rejected immediately with 429 + retry metadata; no timed server delay is applied before response. Contract verification for VAL-USER-071/072/073 is based on measured response timing (~1s/~2s/~5s).

Milestone tests validate a 30-second throttle and 10-attempt lockout (`applies 30-second progressive throttle...`, `applies lockout after 10 failed attempts...`) rather than the required 1s/2s/5s at 3/10/20 attempts, so test coverage is misaligned with the feature contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature provenance is not traceable in mission state: this feature is marked completed but has no worker session linkage/handoff, making it unclear which transcript corresponds to the final implementation under review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows failed-login-tracking with `workerSessionIds: []` and `completedWorkerSessionId: null` (lines 124, 153, 155), and `handoffs/` has no file matching failed-login-tracking.

summary: FAIL — failed-login tracking exists, but throttling policy and tests do not satisfy the specified/contracted behavior for VAL-USER-071 through VAL-USER-073.
